facts.text: Port Chester’s former factory building, now converted into apartments, stands as one of the village’s most recognizable landmarks.
The village library, established in 1876 by Jared V., has served Port Chester’s residents for over a century.
The Port Chester Historical Society works to learn and teach the public about the village’s rich past.
Capitol Theatre, a historic music venue, has hosted icons such as The Grateful Dead, Janis Joplin, The Rolling Stones, David Bowie, Bob Dylan, and The Ramones.
Life Savers Candy Company operated a factory in Port Chester from 1920 until 1984, now a residential building that illustrates the town’s shift from industry to suburb.
The Wappinger people called the land that became Port Chester “Haseco,” meaning marshy land or marshy hassock.
